# rex

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 241
- **Lines of code**: 85,734
- **Languages**: TypeScript (223), JSON (9), Other (4), Markdown (4), Text (1)
- **Zones**: 9 (9 with descriptions)
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-08T02:09:42.003Z

## Architecture Zones

### CLI Command Layer (`cli-command-layer`)

Rex's CLI surface: command handlers for status, move, remove, verify, usage, and adapter management, plus shared output and error utilities.

- Files: 24
- Cohesion: 0.40, Coupling: 0.60
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/token-format.ts, src/cli/commands/usage.ts, src/cli/errors.ts, src/cli/output.ts, src/cli/validate-input.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/cli-command-layer/context.md`

### E2E Tests (`e2e-tests`)

End-to-end CLI test suite covering adapter, analyze, import, prune, quiet mode, recommend, smart-add, sync, and related workflows against real CLI invocations.

- Files: 13
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/e2e-tests/context.md`

### PRD Analysis Pipeline (`prd-analysis-pipeline`)

The core PRD analysis engine: scanning projects, proposing items, deduplicating, diffing, extracting, and reshaping the PRD tree.

- Files: 60
- Cohesion: 0.66, Coupling: 0.34
- Entry points: src/analyze/consolidation-guard.ts, src/analyze/decompose.ts, src/analyze/dedupe.ts, src/analyze/extract.ts, src/analyze/file-validation.ts (+12 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/prd-analysis-pipeline/context.md`

### PRD Store & Sync (`prd-store-sync`)

Data persistence layer responsible for schema definitions, canonical normalization, store contracts, and remote sync adapters (Jira, Notion).

- Files: 45
- Cohesion: 0.47, Coupling: 0.53
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/add.ts, src/cli/commands/fix.ts, src/cli/commands/init.ts, src/cli/commands/sync.ts, src/core/canonical.ts (+13 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/prd-store-sync/context.md`

### PRD Tree Operations (`prd-tree-operations`)

Core PRD runtime engine — tree traversal, DAG integrity, item lifecycle (move, prune, remove), facets, stats, and MCP tool dispatch.

- Files: 64
- Cohesion: 0.57, Coupling: 0.43
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/constants.ts, src/cli/commands/health.ts, src/cli/commands/next.ts, src/cli/commands/reorganize.ts, src/cli/commands/report.ts (+16 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/prd-tree-operations/context.md`

### PRD Validation (`prd-validation`)

Structural integrity checks for the PRD — validate commands, epic-correlation heuristics, and interactive fix prompts — co-located with their unit and integration tests.

- Files: 11
- Cohesion: 0.34, Coupling: 0.66
- Entry points: src/cli/commands/validate.ts, src/core/structural.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/prd-validation/context.md`

### Project Root (`project-root`)

Top-level project configuration, documentation, and build metadata that anchors the monorepo.

- Files: 9
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/project-root/context.md`

### Recommend & Acknowledge (`recommend-acknowledge`)

The recommendation workflow: accepting sourcevision-driven PRD proposals, detecting conflicts, and wiring the recommend CLI command.

- Files: 9
- Cohesion: 0.43, Coupling: 0.57
- Entry points: src/analyze/acknowledge.ts, src/cli/commands/recommend.ts, src/recommend/create-from-recommendations.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/recommend-acknowledge/context.md`

### Rex Runtime State (`rex-runtime-state`)

Persistent runtime artifacts for the rex PRD manager: project config, the PRD tree, workflow state, and execution logs.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/rex-runtime-state/context.md`

## Key Dependencies

### Most-Imported Files

- `src/core/tree.ts` (48 imports)
- `src/schema/index.ts` (44 imports)
- `src/store/index.ts` (31 imports)
- `src/cli/commands/constants.ts` (29 imports)
- `src/cli/output.ts` (27 imports)
- `src/cli/errors.ts` (26 imports)
- `src/analyze/reason.ts` (23 imports)
- `src/core/canonical.ts` (16 imports)
- `src/store/project-config.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/analyze/dedupe.ts` (8 imports)

### External Packages

- `vitest` (used by 114 files)
- `@n-dx/llm-client` (used by 13 files)
- `zod` (used by 7 files)
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (used by 2 files)
- `ora` (used by 1 files)

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- guided.ts owns both the AI clarification protocol (buildClarifyPrompt, clarify) and interactive terminal I/O (promptLine using readline/process.stdin). Embedding process.stdin reading in a domain service makes unit testing impossible without mocking node internals and violates single responsibility — the prompting concern belongs in the CLI command that calls guided.ts. [warning] (prd-analysis-pipeline)
- Schema primitives (v1.ts, levels.ts, validate.ts) and remote sync adapters (sync-engine.ts, adapter-registry.ts, integration-schemas/) have mismatched change rates but share a zone. Schema changes should not trigger re-review of adapter code and vice versa — split into a schema-definitions sub-zone and a sync-adapters sub-zone. [warning] (prd-store-sync)
- 21 public entry points with no enforcing barrel export means the zone has no defined public API — all 64 files are implicitly importable. This is the primary mechanism through which the 98-import unit-core→unit-store coupling accumulates: consumers import whatever internal file is convenient rather than a declared interface. [warning] (prd-tree-operations)
- mcp-tools.ts and mcp.ts are transport-layer files living inside the largest domain zone. A protocol change (e.g. MCP spec update) will require touching domain files, and a domain refactor risks breaking MCP serialization. Extract MCP dispatch to a dedicated transport zone. [warning] (prd-tree-operations)
- prd-validation contains 4 production files that belong to two different architectural layers: epic-correlation.ts and structural.ts are domain utilities (should join prd-tree-operations) while validate.ts and validate-interactive.ts are CLI commands (should join cli). The zone exists as an artifact of import proximity, not architectural intent. [warning] (prd-validation)
- create-from-recommendations.ts re-exports EnrichedRecommendation, RecommendationMeta, and ConflictStrategy from ./types.js solely for backwards compatibility. The project's own CLAUDE.md forbids backwards-compatibility shims; these re-exports should be removed and callers updated to import from the canonical source. [warning] (recommend-acknowledge)
- God function: cmdStatus in src/cli/commands/status.ts calls 36 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Patterns

- The CLI zone functions as an unintended shared vocabulary layer: 30 reverse imports from unit-core (10), unit-store (7), and unit-analyze (13) all reach upward into CLI for utilities and types. This pattern indicates CLI-layer symbols have been promoted to cross-domain status without deliberate design. [warning] (cli-command-layer)
- The single concrete reverse import (guided.ts → cli/output.js) is the root cause of the 13-import unit-analyze → cli relationship. Extracting a Logger or OutputPort interface that CLI implements would sever this inversion without restructuring files. [warning] (prd-analysis-pipeline)
- The three hub files (constants.ts, output.ts, tree.ts) are each imported by 5 zones and all three reside in or near prd-tree-operations; these files act as architectural glue that prevents clean zone boundaries — extracting them into a dedicated shared-primitives layer would reduce fan-in and let zone metrics reflect true domain cohesion [warning] (prd-tree-operations)
- Test files in prd-validation (validate.test.ts, validate-interactive.test.ts, etc.) share a community with production files solely because of import proximity; this contaminates cohesion and coupling scores and should be resolved by moving tests to the unit zone or co-locating them with the unit-cli zone rather than letting them drift into a production-dominated zone [warning] (prd-validation)
- recommend-acknowledge is the only multi-file production zone with no reverse imports flowing back into it from its dependencies, making it a clean consumer zone with a well-defined dependency direction. [info] (recommend-acknowledge)

### Relationships

- CLIError defined in src/cli/errors.ts is imported by src/core/move.ts, making a domain module depend on a CLI module. This inverts the expected flow and should be resolved by moving CLIError to a shared errors or foundation layer. [critical] (cli-command-layer)
- The stark contrast between e2e-tests (cohesion 1.0, coupling 0) and unit-cli (cohesion 0.34) suggests the project's test strategy is bifurcated: end-to-end tests are disciplined and isolated while unit tests have accumulated without a consistent home; a zone-aligned unit test convention (co-locate test files with the zone they cover) would close this gap [info] (e2e-tests)
- unit-analyze participates in a three-zone dependency cycle: unit-core → unit-analyze (22 imports) → unit-store (37 imports) → unit-core (16 imports). This forms a closed loop across three zones and is the most critical structural risk in the codebase. [critical] (prd-analysis-pipeline)
- unit-store → unit-analyze (7 imports) combined with unit-analyze → unit-store (37 imports) forms a second bidirectional loop distinct from the unit-core ↔ unit-store loop; two simultaneous bidirectional cycles in the same zone elevate circular-dependency risk above what either cycle alone would imply [critical] (prd-store-sync)
- Tightly coupled modules: src/cli/commands/prune.ts and src/cli/output.ts — 60 cross-file calls (unidirectional). Consider extracting shared interface or merging [info]

### Observations

- High coupling (0.6) — 30 imports target "prd-tree-operations" [warning] (cli-command-layer)
- Coupling of 0.6 is at the warning threshold; this zone imports from three separate domain zones (unit-core, unit-store, unit-analyze), making it vulnerable to breakage from changes in any of them. [warning] (cli-command-layer)
- Grouping shared CLI utilities (errors.ts, output.ts, validate-input.ts) in src/cli/ rather than scattering them across command files is a clean pattern that should be preserved. [info] (cli-command-layer)
- Reverse imports from unit-core and unit-store back into the cli zone violate the expected unidirectional dependency from CLI → domain; investigate whether domain modules have leaked CLI concerns. [warning] (cli-command-layer)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (e2e-tests)
- Perfect isolation (cohesion 1.0, coupling 0) confirms the e2e suite treats the CLI as a true black box — a strong architectural signal that the public CLI contract is stable and testable without internal access. [info] (e2e-tests)
- cli-recommend.test.ts and cli-smart-add.test.ts target the two most complex commands; ensuring these tests cover failure paths and edge inputs will protect the highest-risk user-facing flows. [info] (e2e-tests)
- Bidirectional coupling: "prd-store-sync" ↔ "prd-tree-operations" (16+98 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Hub: src/cli/commands/constants.ts is imported by 5 zones — cross-cutting dependency [info]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 64 files vs smallest with 4 — uneven decomposition [info]
- ... and 25 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "cli-command-layer" has files across 6 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (cli-command-layer)
- chunked-review-state.ts is an ~870-line pure state machine explicitly documented as having 'no I/O' — move it out of src/cli/commands/ to src/analyze/ or src/review/ to match its domain role. Its current location inflates cli-command-layer's file count and hides a substantial domain concern inside the CLI layer. [warning] (cli-command-layer)
- cli-command-layer is the only zone with BOTH high coupling (0.6) AND low cohesion (0.4) — the combination makes it the most fragile production zone in the codebase. Changes to any of three upstream zones (unit-core, unit-store, unit-analyze) can break it, while its low internal cohesion ensures failures propagate unpredictably. Splitting command files from shared CLI utilities (output.ts, errors.ts, validate-input.ts, token-format.ts) into separate zones would reduce both metrics. [warning] (cli-command-layer)
- token-format.ts should be split into two files: a pure formatting module (formatAggregateTokenUsage, formatBudgetWarnings) and a budget-check orchestration module (preflightBudgetCheck). Mixing IO-bound store access with presentation-only string formatting in a file named 'token-format' violates single responsibility and makes the file's scope invisible from its name. [info] (cli-command-layer)
- src/cli/commands/ contains files from 6 zones (cli-command-layer, prd-analysis-pipeline, prd-store-sync, prd-tree-operations, prd-validation, recommend-acknowledge) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- Adopt a single zone naming convention: either all zones named by domain concern (prd-*, recommend-*) or all zones named by layer (cli-*, store-*, core-*). The current mixture of both taxonomies makes zone IDs non-comparable and forces developers to inspect zone contents to understand naming rationale. [info]
- Establish a zone naming convention in CLAUDE.md or an ADR: e.g. 'src-*' for production domain zones, 'cli-*' for CLI-layer zones, 'test-*' for test zones, 'data-*' for config/data zones. Current mixed naming (prd-*, unit-*, e2e-tests, cli, root) makes architectural category ambiguous and will worsen as new zones are created. [info]
- No production zone achieves the target quadrant (cohesion > 0.7, coupling < 0.3). Prioritize extracting a pure 'schema-definitions' zone (v1.ts, levels.ts from prd-store-sync) as a concrete architectural reference point — it is the only candidate with a stable change rate, no logic dependencies, and a natural extraction boundary. [info]
- Root-level context.md and llms.txt at the project root coexist with .sourcevision/CONTEXT.md and .sourcevision/llms.txt — two sets of AI-readable context files with no enforcement that they stay in sync. Add a CI step that either removes the root copies or verifies they match the sourcevision-generated output to prevent AI tools from consuming stale context. [warning]
- Run `madge --circular packages/rex/src` to enumerate all circular dependency paths before the next architectural refactor. Known bidirectional zone pairs are pairwise observations only — three-node or longer cycles (e.g. unit-core → unit-analyze → cli → unit-core via transitive imports) cannot be detected from zone metrics and may already exist. [info]
- ... and 21 more

| `tsconfig.json` | config |  | project-root |
| `vitest.config.ts` | config |  | project-root |

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
